basic_details.job_title: NIAB Technical Officer Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online
basic_details.short_information: NIAB Recruitment 2026 notification (Advertisement No. 16/2026) has been released by the National Institute of Animal Biotechnology (NIAB), Hyderabad, for 1 vacancy of Technical Officer under the Unreserved (UR) category. NIAB, an autonomous institute under the Department of Biotechnology, Ministry of Science & Technology, Government of India, has invited online applications from suitably qualified Indian citizens from 13 July 2026, with the last date for submission being 14 August 2026. This article covers complete details of NIAB Recruitment 2026, including eligibility criteria, age limit, pa

application_fee.details: Candidates are required to pay the following category-wise application fee for the Technical Officer post: | PWD and departmental candidates are exempted from payment of the application fee. | The fee can be paid in either of the following ways: | A crossed Demand Draft drawn on any nationalised bank, valid for at least 3 months, in favour of "National Institute of Animal Biotechnology, Hyderabad", payable at Hyderabad, preferably Punjab National Bank, Gachibowli (Code No. PUNB0498700); or Direct online payment through the payment link: https://epayments.in.worldline.com/niab

age_limit.details: The upper age limit for the Technical Officer post is 35 years. Age will be reckoned as on the closing date for receipt of the online application. Age relaxation will be applicable as per Government of India guidelines. Reservation for Persons with Disability (PWD) will be in accordance with Section 33 of the Disabilities Act, 1995.

qualification.details: Candidates must be Indian citizens and fulfil the following educational qualification, experience, and age criteria to be eligible for the NIAB Technical Officer post. | Candidates must possess any one of the following essential qualifications: | B.Sc. with 7 years of relevant experience, or B.Tech / B.V.Sc. / M.Sc. with 3 years of relevant experience | Desirable qualifications for the post include: | Experience of laboratory training focused on complex laboratory techniques, advanced instrumental analysis, and advanced animal cell culture techniques Experience working in laminar hoods/bio-safety cabinets and performing molecular biology techniques such as RT-PCR, Western blotting, and gene silencing Proficiency in handling inverted microscopes, CO2 incubators, cell counters, and similar laboratory equipment Knowledge of bio-analytical techniques, laboratory safety, and quality assurance Proficiency in statistical and computer productivity software such as Word and Excel | Experience for the position will be reckoned from the date of acquiring the prescribed qualification. | The selected Technical Officer will be responsible for assisting Scientists in laboratory-related work, or any other job assigned by the competent authority. The role requires strong team-working skills, and duties will primarily be performed as per laboratory guidelines and procedures.

salary_details.details: The Technical Officer post carries Pay Level 6 as per the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC), along with usual allowances admissible to Central Government employees of the same pay and status posted at Hyderabad, Telangana. | Appointment will initially be on probation for a period of 2 years, which may be extended at the discretion of the Competent Authority, and further continuation will depend on the candidate's performance during probation.

selection_process: Only shortlisted candidates will be called for a Written Test and/or Skill Test
as decided by the Competent Authority. If a large number of applications are received

screening will be done to shortlist candidates possessing higher/relevant qualifications and experience. The Institute reserves the right to convert desirable qualifications into essential qualifications to optimise the number of candidates called for the selection process.
Candidates called for the written test and/or skill test will be paid single second-class rail fare from the actual place of journey or normal place of residence
whichever is nearer to Secunderabad Railway Station
on production of relevant travel documents.
how_to_apply: Eligible candidates can apply for the NIAB Technical Officer post by following these steps:
Visit the official NIAB website at www.niab.res.in
New candidates must register on the website with a username, password, and a valid e-mail ID. Already registered candidates may log in with their existing username and password
Fill in the online application form with accurate personal, educational, and experience details
Upload the required documents (photograph, educational certificates, experience certificates, caste/category certificate, NOC, and other testimonials) in the relevant sections of the application form
Pay the prescribed application fee through Demand Draft or the online payment link, as applicable
Upload the self-attested copies of certificates, mark sheets, testimonials, curriculum vitae, and fee payment receipt/DD copy in PDF format
Submit the application on or before the last date; note that a hard copy is not required
Take a printout of the submitted online application (PDF) and retain it for future reference
Applications without signature, received after the due date and time, incomplete in any aspect, or submitted without fee payment will be summarily rejected.
important_instructions: Only Indian citizens can apply for this post. Women candidates fulfilling the eligibility requirements are encouraged to apply. Canvassing in any form, or bringing any political or other influence, will automatically disqualify the candidate. Mere fulfilment of the minimum qualification and experience does not entitle a candidate to be called for a written/skill test; the decision of the Competent Authority is final in this regard. The Institute reserves the right to reject any or all applications and to cancel the advertised post at any stage of the selection process, without assigning any reason. Incomplete applications are not likely to be considered. No interim enquiries about the recruitment will be entertained. For any legal dispute, the courts of law at Hyderabad, Telangana will have jurisdiction. For technical queries while filling the application, candidates may e-mail [email protected].

content_sections.heading: NIAB Recruitment 2026 Notification
content_sections.text: NIAB Recruitment 2026 offers a valuable opportunity for qualified science and biotechnology graduates to join as Technical Officer at a premier autonomous research institute under the Department of Biotechnology, Government of India. NIAB Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: NIAB Recruitment 2026: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates are advised to apply early and not wait for the last date, to avoid last-moment internet traffic issues on the application portal.

rows.event: Date of commencement of online applications
Last date for receipt of online applications

content_sections.heading: NIAB Technical Officer Vacancy Details 2026
content_sections.text: NIAB has notified 1 vacancy for the post of Technical Officer under the Unreserved (UR) category. This is a Lien vacancy filled through Direct Recruitment, which is likely to be continued.

content_sections.heading: NIAB Technical Officer Eligibility Criteria 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates must be Indian citizens and fulfil the following educational qualification, experience, and age criteria to be eligible for the NIAB Technical Officer post. Candidates must possess any one of the following essential qualifications: B.Sc. with 7 years of relevant experience, or B.Tech / B.V.Sc. / M.Sc. with 3 years of relevant experience Desirable qualifications for the post include: Experience of laboratory training focused on complex laboratory techniques, advanced instrumental analysis, and advanced animal cell culture techniques Experience working in laminar hoods/bio-safety cabinets and performing molecular biology techniques such as RT-PCR, Western blotting, and gene silencing Proficiency in handling inverted microscopes, CO2 incubators, cell counters, and similar laboratory equipment Knowledge of bio-analytical techniques, laboratory safety, and quality assurance Proficiency in statistical and computer productivity software such as Word and Excel Experience for the position will be reckoned from the date of acquiring the prescribed qualification. The selected Technical Officer will be responsible for assisting Scientists in laboratory-related work, or any other job assigned by the competent authority. The role requires strong team-working skills, and duties will primarily be performed as per laboratory guidelines and procedures.

content_sections.heading: Age Limit
content_sections.text: The upper age limit for the Technical Officer post is 35 years. Age will be reckoned as on the closing date for receipt of the online application. Age relaxation will be applicable as per Government of India guidelines. Reservation for Persons with Disability (PWD) will be in accordance with Section 33 of the Disabilities Act, 1995.

content_sections.heading: NIAB Technical Officer Salary / Pay Level 2026
content_sections.text: The Technical Officer post carries Pay Level 6 as per the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC), along with usual allowances admissible to Central Government employees of the same pay and status posted at Hyderabad, Telangana. Appointment will initially be on probation for a period of 2 years, which may be extended at the discretion of the Competent Authority, and further continuation will depend on the candidate's performance during probation.

content_sections.heading: NIAB Recruitment 2026 Application Fee
content_sections.text: Candidates are required to pay the following category-wise application fee for the Technical Officer post: PWD and departmental candidates are exempted from payment of the application fee. The fee can be paid in either of the following ways: A crossed Demand Draft drawn on any nationalised bank, valid for at least 3 months, in favour of "National Institute of Animal Biotechnology, Hyderabad", payable at Hyderabad, preferably Punjab National Bank, Gachibowli (Code No. PUNB0498700); or Direct online payment through the payment link: https://epayments.in.worldline.com/niab

rows.category: Unreserved candidates from abroad with OCI status

content_sections.heading: NIAB Technical Officer Selection Process 2026
content_sections.text: Only shortlisted candidates will be called for a Written Test and/or Skill Test, as decided by the Competent Authority. If a large number of applications are received, screening will be done to shortlist candidates possessing higher/relevant qualifications and experience. The Institute reserves the right to convert desirable qualifications into essential qualifications to optimise the number of candidates called for the selection process. Candidates called for the written test and/or skill test will be paid single second-class rail fare from the actual place of journey or normal place of residence, whichever is nearer to Secunderabad Railway Station, on production of relevant travel documents.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for NIAB Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Before applying online, candidates should keep the following soft copies ready in PDF/JPEG format: Recent passport-size photograph All educational certificates All experience certificates Caste/Category certificate, if applicable No Objection Certificate (NOC) from present employer, for candidates working in Government/Semi-Government Organizations/Autonomous Bodies/PSUs Fee payment receipt / Demand Draft copy (PDF format) Curriculum Vitae Any other relevant certificates or testimonials Candidates employed in Government/Semi-Government Organizations, Autonomous Bodies, or PSUs must route their application through proper channel and should also submit a certificate from their employer at the time of written/skill test confirming that no vigilance/disciplinary case is pending or contemplated against them.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for NIAB Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Eligible candidates can apply for the NIAB Technical Officer post by following these steps: Visit the official NIAB website at www.niab.res.in New candidates must register on the website with a username, password, and a valid e-mail ID. Already registered candidates may log in with their existing username and password Fill in the online application form with accurate personal, educational, and experience details Upload the required documents (photograph, educational certificates, experience certificates, caste/category certificate, NOC, and other testimonials) in the relevant sections of the application form Pay the prescribed application fee through Demand Draft or the online payment link, as applicable Upload the self-attested copies of certificates, mark sheets, testimonials, curriculum vitae, and fee payment receipt/DD copy in PDF format Submit the application on or before the last date; note that a hard copy is not required Take a printout of the submitted online application (PDF) and retain it for future reference Applications without signature, received after the due date and time, incomplete in any aspect, or submitted without fee payment will be summarily rejected.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions for NIAB Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Only Indian citizens can apply for this post. Women candidates fulfilling the eligibility requirements are encouraged to apply. Canvassing in any form, or bringing any political or other influence, will automatically disqualify the candidate. Mere fulfilment of the minimum qualification and experience does not entitle a candidate to be called for a written/skill test; the decision of the Competent Authority is final in this regard. The Institute reserves the right to reject any or all applications and to cancel the advertised post at any stage of the selection process, without assigning any reason. Incomplete applications are not likely to be considered. No interim enquiries about the recruitment will be entertained. For any legal dispute, the courts of law at Hyderabad, Telangana will have jurisdiction. For technical queries while filling the application, candidates may e-mail [email protected] .

faq.question: Q1. What is NIAB Recruitment 2026 about?
faq.answer: NIAB Recruitment 2026 (Advertisement No. 16/2026) is a notification inviting online applications for 1 vacancy of Technical Officer at the National Institute of Animal Biotechnology, Hyderabad.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in NIAB Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There is 1 vacancy notified for the Technical Officer post, under the Unreserved (UR) category.

faq.question: Q3. What is the last date to apply for NIAB Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for receipt of online applications is 14 August 2026, by 5 PM.

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification required for NIAB Technical Officer post?
faq.answer: Candidates must have a B.Sc. with 7 years of experience, or a B.Tech/B.V.Sc./M.Sc. with 3 years of experience.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for NIAB Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 35 years, reckoned as on the closing date of the online application, with age relaxation as per Government of India guidelines.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary/pay level for the NIAB Technical Officer post?
faq.answer: The post carries Pay Level 6 as per the 7th CPC, along with usual allowances admissible to Central Government employees at Hyderabad.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application fee for NIAB Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is Rs. 200 for Unreserved candidates and Rs. 100 for SC/ST/OBC/Women candidates. PWD and departmental candidates are exempted from the fee.

faq.question: Q8. How can I apply for NIAB Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply online through www.niab.res.in by registering on the portal, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and paying the applicable fee.

faq.question: Q9. Is a hard copy of the application required for NIAB Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No, hard copy is not required. All documents, including the fee payment receipt/DD copy, must be uploaded online in PDF format.

faq.question: Q10. Where is the NIAB Technical Officer job location?
faq.answer: The job location is the National Institute of Animal Biotechnology, Gachibowli, Hyderabad, Telangana.
